MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC. and CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE CO., DBA ABLE BUILDING's Motion To Seal In Connection With Plaintiff's Oppositions To Defendants' Motion In Limine.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed that specifies the documents to be sealed.
